# Tina4 Framework — LLM Context

Tina4 is a multi-language web framework with 54 built-in features and zero dependencies.
Available for Python, PHP, Ruby, and Node.js. Frontend library: tina4-js.
Website: https://tina4.com | GitHub: https://github.com/tina4stack

---

## Quick Start (All Languages)

```bash
# Install the CLI
pip install tina4-python     # Python
composer require tina4stack/tina4php  # PHP
gem install tina4-ruby       # Ruby
npm install tina4-nodejs     # Node.js

# Scaffold a project
tina4 init

# Start dev server
tina4 serve
```

Default ports: Python 7145, PHP 7146, Ruby 7147, Node.js 7148

---

## Route Registration

### Python
```python
from tina4_python.core.router import get, post, noauth

@get("/api/users")
async def list_users(request, response):
    return response({"users": []})

@post("/api/users")
@noauth()  # POST requires auth by default
async def create(request, response):
    return response({"created": request.body["name"]}, 201)
```

### PHP
```php
use Tina4\Router;

Router::get("/api/users", function($request, $response) {
    return $response->json(["users" => []], 200);
});

Router::post("/api/users", function($request, $response) {
    return $response->json(["created" => $request->body["name"]], 201);
})->noAuth();
```

### Ruby
```ruby
Tina4::Router.get "/api/users" do |request, response|
  response.json({ users: [] })
end

Tina4::Router.post("/api/users") { |req, res|
  res.json({ created: req.body["name"] })
}.no_auth
```

### Node.js (TypeScript)
```typescript
import { Router } from "@tina4/core";

Router.get("/api/users", async (req, res) => {
  return res.json({ users: [] });
});

// Or file-based: src/routes/api/users/get.ts
export default async function (req, res) {
  return res.json({ users: [] });
}
```

---

## Database

All languages use URL-based connection strings:
- `sqlite:///app.db`
- `postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb`
- `mysql://user:pass@localhost:3306/mydb`
- `mssql://user:pass@localhost:1433/mydb`
- `firebird://user:pass@localhost:3050/mydb`

---

## ORM

### Python
```python
from tina4_python.orm import ORM, IntegerField, StringField

class User(ORM):
    id = IntegerField(primary_key=True, auto_increment=True)
    name = StringField()
```

### PHP
```php
class User extends \Tina4\ORM {
    public $tableName = "users";
    public $id;
    public $name;
}
```

### Ruby
```ruby
class User < Tina4::ORM
  integer_field :id, primary_key: true, auto_increment: true
  string_field :name
  table_name "users"
end
```

### Node.js
```typescript
export default class User {
  static tableName = "users";
  static fields = {
    id:   { type: "integer" as const, primaryKey: true, autoIncrement: true },
    name: { type: "string" as const,  required: true },
  };
}
```

---

## Auth (All Languages)

- JWT token generation and validation built in
- Password hashing via PBKDF2/bcrypt built in
- POST/PUT/DELETE routes require auth by default
- GET routes are public by default
- Python: `@noauth()` / `@secured()`
- PHP: `->noAuth()` / `->secure()`
- Ruby: `.no_auth` / `.secure`
- Node.js: `/** @noauth */` / `/** @secure */` JSDoc

---

## Queue (All Languages)

All languages share the same queue API pattern:
- `queue.push(data)` — enqueue a job
- `queue.consume(topic)` — process jobs
- `job.payload` — access job data (NOT `job.data`)
- `job.complete()` / `job.fail(reason)` — finish a job
- Backends: file (default), RabbitMQ, Kafka, MongoDB

---

## tina4-js (Frontend)

Sub-3KB reactive JavaScript framework. Shipped with all Tina4 backends at `/js/tina4js.min.js`.

**CRITICAL: Signals use .value property access:**
```javascript
const count = signal(0);
count.value = 5;       // write (NOT count(5))
count.value;           // read  (NOT count())

// In templates: pass signal itself for reactive binding
html`<p>${count}</p>`          // RIGHT — reactive
html`<p>${count.value}</p>`    // WRONG — evaluates once, freezes
```

Key rules:
- Boolean attributes: `?disabled=${loading}` (NOT `disabled=${loading}`)
- Conditionals: `${() => cond ? html\`...\` : null}` (NOT `${cond && html\`...\`}`)
- Arrays: `items.value = [...items.value, newItem]` (NOT `.push()`)
- Events: `@click=${fn}` (NOT `onclick=${fn}`)
- Route: `route(pattern, handler)` — pattern first
- API: `api.configure({baseUrl})` then `api.get()` — singleton
- WebSocket: `ws.connect(url)` — not `ws(url)`

---

## Project Structure (All Languages)

```
src/
  routes/       # Auto-discovered route files
  orm/          # ORM model definitions (models/ in Node.js)
  templates/    # Frond/Twig templates
  public/       # Static files served at /
migrations/     # SQL migration files
.env            # Environment variables
```

---

## Common Gotchas

1. Queue job payload: `job.payload` NOT `job.data` (all languages)
2. Database pagination: `offset` NOT `skip` (all languages)
3. Python routes return `response()` NOT `response.json()`
4. PHP route methods are static: `Router::get()` NOT `$router->get()`
5. Ruby route params: `{id}` NOT `:id`
6. Node.js is ESM only — use `.js` extensions in TypeScript imports
7. tina4-js signals: `.value` property NOT function calls
8. Templates use Twig syntax across all backends
